channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Invincible VS Developer Skybound Exits Third-Party Publishing
item.content:encoded:
Before Your Eyes and Goodnight Universe publisher (not to mention Invincible VS developer) Skybound Games has announced that it's exiting the publishing sphere, choosing instead to focus on its own IPs.

In a conversation with The Game Business' Christopher Dring, Skybound's Jon Goldman says that the studio has "learned a lot in indie publishing", but that it's time to be "smarter" about the games it brings to market. The studio will be spending its resources on its own IPs henceforth, Goldman says.

Fellow Skybound head David Alpert says the studio was able to "find some interesting opportunities" in the third-party publishing space, but that bringing brand new IPs into the market in this day and age, while "completely possible", is also "really hard".

Goldman says that Invincible VS, the upcoming fighting game from Skybound and newly-minted internal dev team Quarter Up, "has to be something [Skybound's] fans love and [the team is] really proud of", and it's not always possible to achieve that by "offloading [the project] to someone else".

As such, Dring says Skybound is investing heavily in Invincible VS. Alpert says that Invincible, along with The Walking Dead, are properties that the studio believes "are going to be around for decades to come".

Prior to its exit from the publishing world, Skybound was responsible for publishing indie curios like GoodbyeWorldGames' Before Your Eyes, Nice Dream's Goodnight Universe, and the irreverent wrestling RPG WrestleQuest, as well as the Paper Mario-esque adventure Rainbow Billy: The Curse of the Leviathan.

While Skybound might be leaving publishing, that doesn't mean the games and franchises it's published will be going away; in fact, Rainbow Billy is getting itself a sequel, The Book of Fears, sometime in the future, with publishing being handled by developer Manavoid alongside Indie Asylum.

Invincible VS launches tomorrow for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S, so we'll have to wait and see whether it's a worthy place for Skybound to put all of its efforts. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
